us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran as Military Losses and Diplomacy Collide
us-politics.body: President Trump warned Iran that 'time is running out' before major retaliation, even as the US confirmed the loss of fighter jets to Iranian fire. Rep. Seth Moulton argued Iran is effectively winning the confrontation, while former National Security Advisor John Bolton suggested Trump may be in 'panic mode' following the losses. World leaders are meanwhile moving to manage Strait of Hormuz tensions independently, largely bypassing Washington. The divergence between Trump's escalatory rhetoric and allies seeking their own diplomatic track reflects a widening gap in strategy. Critics on the left question what a US endgame looks like, while hawks on the right push for decisive action. Neither side has offered a clear answer on what 'finishing the job' would actually require.

sources.title: In Iran, what does ‘finish the job’ even mean?
Trump warns Iran: ‘Time is running out’ before ‘all hell’ rains down
Moulton says ‘Iran is winning the war’ after US jets downed
Bolton: Trump likely in ‘panic mode’ after Iran attacks US fighter jets
World leaders bypass Trump to tackle Strait of Hormuz crisis

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Cuts Domestic Spending, Boosts Defense and Prisons
us-politics.body: The Trump administration released a budget proposal seeking a $1.5 trillion increase in defense spending while making deep cuts to domestic programs. The plan also includes $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a high-security federal prison, a move critics dismissed as symbolic. Child care advocates pushed back hard against a separate proposal to shift federal day care funding to states, warning it would reduce access for low-income families. Supporters argue the defense buildup is overdue and that devolving domestic programs gives states more flexibility. Opponents say the trade-off hits working families hardest. The budget sets up a sharp fight in Congress, where even some Republicans have reservations about the scale of domestic cuts.

sources.title: Trump unveils $1.5T defense surge, deep domestic cuts — what’s on the budget chopping block
Alcatraz could reopen as a 'state-of-the-art secure prison' under Trump's $152M budget request
Trump floats letting states handle day care. Advocates call it a lead balloon

us-politics.headline: Republicans Eye Tough Midterms as Cabinet Shake-Ups Continue
us-politics.body: Former White House press secretary Kayleigh McEnany warned publicly that the 2026 midterms will be difficult for Republicans, a rare candid admission from within the party's media orbit. The departures of Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem from the Cabinet have fuelled speculation about further shake-ups, raising questions about internal stability heading into an election cycle. On the Democratic side, Rep. Eric Swalwell's clashes with the FBI are drawing scrutiny as he eyes a California governor run, with opponents arguing the controversy could dog his campaign. Virginia polling shows narrow public support for redistricting maps that would favour Democrats, a sign both parties are already maneuvering for 2026 advantage.

sources.title: McEnany sounds alarm: Midterms ‘will be hard’ for Republicans
Swalwell-FBI clash draws attention to Trump foe’s California governor bid
Narrow majority in Virginia backs redistricting to favor Democrats: Poll
Bondi, Noem ousters raise questions about future Cabinet shake-ups

us-politics.headline: Birthright Citizenship Debate Reaches Courts and Congress
us-politics.body: The Supreme Court is weighing the Trump administration's challenge to birthright citizenship, a case that could reshape a constitutional guarantee in place since 1868. A separate federal charge against the child of Chinese undocumented immigrants accused of planting an explosive at a US military base has intensified political debate around immigration enforcement and national security. Some commentators argue a constitutional amendment may be the only clean resolution to the birthright question, while civil liberties advocates warn any rollback would affect hundreds of thousands of people born on US soil. The cases together are pushing immigration back to the centre of national debate well ahead of the midterms.

sources.title: Reclaiming our own birthright: We might need to amend the Constitution
Child of Chinese illegal immigrants charged with planting explosive at US military base
Week in Politics: Trump's latest speech; SCOTUS on birthright citizenship; Pam Bondi

au-politics.headline: Murder charge, missing women dominate Australian crime headlines
au-politics.body:
A man was charged with murder after police intercepted him attempting to board an international flight, connecting him to a missing woman near Little River. Separately, Victoria Police arrested two people in connection with the Dezi Freeman investigation, a case that has drawn sustained public attention. In Western Australia, a full-scale search is underway for Naomi Marie Duckett, missing near The Gap in Albany - a popular tourism site that presents significant terrain challenges for searchers.

The three cases reflect a busy period for Australian law enforcement across multiple states. The Little River arrest is particularly striking given the attempted flight, suggesting investigators moved quickly to prevent the suspect leaving the country. No further charges have been announced in the Freeman case.

sources.title: Man trying to board international flight charged with murder
Victoria police arrest two people as part of Dezi Freeman investigation
Police launch full-scale search to locate woman missing in popular tourism spot

au-politics.headline: Confidential report labels ex-Home Affairs chief Pezzullo 'reckless'
au-politics.body:
A confidential report has found former Home Affairs secretary Michael Pezzullo acted recklessly in his dealings with a Liberal Party powerbroker, adding fresh detail to the conduct that led to his dismissal. Pezzullo was one of Australia's most powerful public servants before being sacked in 2023 following revelations of political text exchanges.

The report's findings raise ongoing questions about the boundaries between senior bureaucrats and political operatives. Pezzullo has previously acknowledged the texts but contested characterisations of his conduct. The confidential nature of the report means full public scrutiny remains limited, and it is unclear whether further action will follow.

sources.title: Confidential report found former home affairs boss Michael Pezzullo was ‘reckless’ in engagement with Liberal powerbroker

au-politics.headline: Victorian election takes shape as challenger targets Premier Allan
au-politics.body:
Andrew Lethlean has confirmed he will contest Bendigo East against Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan at the next state election, setting up a direct challenge to the state's top political office-holder in her own seat. Targeting a sitting premier in their electorate is a high-risk, high-reward strategy - it forces the government to divert resources to a seat that would otherwise be safe.

Allan has held Bendigo East since 2002 and succeeded Daniel Andrews as premier in 2023. Lethlean's candidacy signals the opposition sees an opportunity to apply pressure, though unseating a long-serving premier in a home seat remains a difficult task historically.

sources.title: Lethlean to challenge Victorian premier in Bendigo East

world.headline: US-Israel War on Iran Escalates With Nuclear Plant Strike
world.body:
A projectile struck near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one person, as the IAEA voiced deep concern over attacks on Iranian nuclear facilities. Israel and the US have been conducting strikes on Iran, hitting a Tehran university and drawing a furious Iranian response. Tehran called the two countries relics of the Stone Age.

Trump gave Iran a 48-hour ultimatum to reach a deal, while the US simultaneously arrested relatives of slain Iranian general Qasem Soleimani, ratcheting up pressure on multiple fronts. A US airman remains missing inside Iran after a military incident, with rescue teams describing the search as harrowing. Iran executed two members of a banned opposition group amid the conflict, signalling it will not soften domestically under external pressure.

sources.title: Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal as search for US pilot continues
UN watchdog voices 'deep concern' as Iran reports new attacks on nuclear plant
US arrests relatives of deceased Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
Iran says US, Israel belong in Stone Age after Tehran university strike
Projectile hits near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one: IAEA
What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group

world.headline: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Ground Invasion Advances
world.body:
Israeli forces struck a hospital in the southern Lebanese city of Tyre and hit a residential building there, as a ground invasion continued to push forward. Witnesses recorded strikes on civilian infrastructure, and Iranian projectile debris caused vehicle fires inside Israel, illustrating how the regional conflict is spreading across borders.

Lebanon's displacement crisis deepens as hundreds of thousands remain unable to return home. Families describe lives turned upside down, with no clear timeline for when the fighting will stop.

sources.title: Witness records Israeli strike on building in Lebanon’s Tyre
Vehicles burn in Israel after being hit with Iranian projectile debris
Israeli strikes damage hospital in Lebanon’s Tyre; ground invasion advances
Displaced in Lebanon: 'Lives turned upside down'

world.headline: Russia Kills 15 in Ukraine During Easter Weekend Offensive
world.body:
Russian attacks killed at least 15 people across Ukraine over the Easter weekend, including five at a market strike. President Zelensky accused Russia of choosing escalation over a ceasefire, meeting Turkish President Erdogan as he sought diplomatic support. The strikes came despite international calls for a holiday pause in fighting.

Zelensky's talks with Erdogan signal continued Ukrainian efforts to build coalitions against Russian pressure, with Ankara maintaining its role as a key intermediary between the two sides.

sources.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
At least 15 killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine as Zelenskyy meets Erdogan
Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky

world.headline: Trump Pushes Alcatraz Reopening and Wilderness Drilling
world.body:
President Trump requested $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a functioning prison, reviving a facility shuttered in 1963. The move fits a broader pattern of provocative domestic policy signals rather than immediate legislative action. It drew swift ridicule but also attention away from substantive policy fights.

Separately, the Trump administration moved to open protected wilderness areas to oil and gas extraction under its drill-baby-drill agenda. Critics warn the policy will cause lasting environmental damage, while supporters frame it as energy independence. US-South Africa relations have also soured sharply, adding to a growing list of strained bilateral ties under the current administration.

sources.title: Trump seeks $152m to reopen notorious Alcatraz prison
'Drill baby drill': Trump opens wilderness to big energy
Why have US-South Africa relations soured?

ai.headline: Anthropic Locks OpenClaw Out of Claude Code Subscriptions
ai.body:
Anthropic has moved to effectively block OpenClaw from its Claude Code platform, requiring subscribers to pay extra if they want to use the tool. The Verge frames it bluntly as a ban. The decision comes as OpenClaw faces serious scrutiny on its own: Ars Technica reports users should assume their systems are compromised, and Wired found hackers distributing the leaked Claude Code source with malware bundled in.

The timing is awkward for Anthropic, which is riding high in private markets. TechCrunch notes the company is attracting strong investor interest, though SpaceX's potential IPO could pull capital away. Meanwhile, Claude Code earned a rare positive headline after a developer documented it uncovering a Linux vulnerability that had gone undetected for 23 years.

sources.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years
Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security

ai.headline: AI Reshapes Thinking, Work, and Who Gets Paid
ai.body:
New research finds that heavy AI use is pushing people toward "cognitive surrender" - a pattern where users stop applying independent reasoning and simply defer to whatever the model says. Ars Technica calls the findings alarming. Separately, a study on code generation shows that simple self-distillation techniques meaningfully improve model output, suggesting the gap between current and better AI may be smaller than assumed.

On the business side, OpenAI reshuffled its executive ranks, giving COO Brad Lightcap a new "special projects" mandate. Elon Musk, meanwhile, is reportedly pressuring banks working on the SpaceX IPO to purchase Grok subscriptions - a move that blurs the line between business leverage and product promotion.

sources.title: Simple self-distillation improves code generation
"Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds
OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions

ai.headline: Apple Clears Nvidia eGPUs for Arm Macs
ai.body:
Apple has approved a driver enabling Nvidia external GPUs to work with Arm-based Macs, ending a long hardware compatibility gap. The move matters most for developers and creative professionals who want more graphics headroom without switching machines. It also signals a quiet thaw in the Apple-Nvidia relationship, which has been largely absent since Apple dropped Nvidia chips from its lineup years ago.

For those who want GPU access without the hardware cost, a new tool called sllm lets developers split a GPU node and run inference with no token limits. Separately, a browser-based port of Google's TurboQuant vector quantization library landed on GitHub, pushing more ML tooling into client-side environments.

sources.title: Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs
Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens
Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser

ai.headline: Trump's Tariffs Undercut His Own AI Ambitions
ai.body:
The administration's push to build out AI data center capacity in the US is stalling, and the cause is largely self-inflicted. Ars Technica reports that Trump's own tariffs are driving up the cost of the hardware and construction materials needed to build the facilities he is publicly championing. The contradiction has gone largely unaddressed by the White House.

On the space front, the picture is more mixed. Artemis II is en route to the Moon and reporting no major issues. But Trump's proposed NASA budget would cut the agency steeply, raising questions about how future missions get funded even as the current one succeeds.

sources.title: Trump proposes steep cut to NASA budget as astronauts head for the Moon
As Artemis II zooms to the Moon, everything seems to be going swimmingly
Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing

btc.headline: Bitcoin Consolidates as Bears Face $2.5B Liquidation Threat
btc.body:
Bitcoin is grinding sideways, but the pressure building underneath could force a violent move. Short sellers are sitting on $2.5 billion in positions that would be wiped out if price reaches $72,000, putting bears in a precarious spot. Analysts say the directionless action is typical before a sharp breakout in either direction.

The market's internal structure is weakening. Five separate data sources point to thinning liquidity, suggesting the order book can support less volume than it appears. Meanwhile, large traders had a brutal first quarter, losing an average of $337 million per day. The combination of fragile liquidity and overleveraged shorts makes the next major move potentially severe.

sources.title: Bitcoin shorts risk $2.5 billion liquidation at $72K: Are bears in danger?
Five data sources say the same thing about bitcoin market. It's thinning from the inside
Rich Bitcoin traders lost $337M daily in first quarter of 2026
Bitcoin’s ‘no direction’ action may lead to heavier breakout: Analyst

btc.headline: Kalshi Blocked Again as Prediction Markets Hit Legal Walls
btc.body:
A Nevada judge extended the ban on Kalshi's sports event contracts, rejecting the platform's argument that its products qualify as legal event contracts rather than unlicensed gambling. The ruling is a significant setback for prediction markets seeking to operate outside traditional gambling frameworks in the United States.

The legal pressure is not limited to Nevada. Prediction markets are running into similar resistance across strict Asian jurisdictions, where regulators are drawing hard lines around what constitutes gambling. Polymarket added to the industry's reputational headaches this week, pulling down a market tied to a missing US pilot after public backlash. The sector is expanding fast but its legal foundations remain contested on multiple fronts.

sources.title: Nevada judge extends ban on Kalshi, rejects event contract defense
Prediction markets are testing legal limits in strict Asian markets
Polymarket takes down market on missing US pilot after backlash
Judge continues Nevada ban on Kalshi sports markets

btc.headline: Ex-UK Chancellor Backs Bitcoin as Mainstream Systems Falter
btc.body:
A former UK Chancellor has publicly endorsed Bitcoin as a credible alternative to institutions he says are failing ordinary people. The endorsement adds political weight to a growing argument that Bitcoin is not a speculative asset but a structural hedge against systemic dysfunction.

The timing matters. A separate analysis argues that corporate digital asset treasuries can no longer justify holding crypto passively and must generate yield to earn their place on balance sheets. Together, the signals suggest Bitcoin is moving from a fringe bet to a tool that serious institutions and policymakers are being forced to reckon with seriously.

sources.title: Ex-UK Chancellor backs bitcoin as alternative to failing systems
Digital asset treasuries must now earn their keep

btc.headline: 24/7 Stock Trading Arrives, Blurring Lines With Crypto
btc.body:
Round-the-clock stock trading is finally becoming a reality, and traders stand to benefit most. The shift promises to eliminate the after-hours price gaps that critics have long called a form of manipulation, where news breaks when markets are closed and retail investors absorb the damage at the open.

For crypto markets, which have operated 24/7 since inception, the development is a form of validation. It also narrows one of the few structural advantages crypto trading venues held over traditional finance. As equities adopt continuous trading, the operational differences between the two asset classes keep shrinking.

sources.title: Traders are the big winners as 24/7 stocks will finally end the after-hours price 'manipulation'

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Walnut Tart
food.description: A buttery shortcrust shell filled with slow-caramelised leeks, white miso, crème fraîche, and toasted walnuts for a savoury umami depth.
food.tip: Sweat the leeks on the lowest heat for at least 25 minutes until they collapse into silky ribbons — rushing this step kills the sweetness.

exercise.name: The Saturday Ladder
exercise.equipment: A single kettlebell (16–24 kg) and a timer
exercise.description: Ascending and descending rep ladders of kettlebell swings, goblet squats, and push-ups with 90-second rest caps, building cardiovascular endurance and full-body strength without a gym.

learn.topic: Eigenvalues and the Google PageRank Algorithm
learn.description: PageRank — the algorithm that made Google — is essentially an eigenvector problem: the internet is modelled as a giant matrix and the 'importance' of each page is the principal eigenvector of that matrix.
learn.why: Understanding this collapses the gap between abstract linear algebra and the infrastructure of the modern web, and once you see it you'll never look at a search result the same way again.
